channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Police investigate Melbourne house fire that left one dead and another injured
item.description: A person has died in a house fire in Werribee and another person has been taken to hospital with serious injuries.

media:group.media:description: A person has died in a house fire in Werribee, in Melbourne's south-west.

item.title: Raped, humiliated, shamed: Women reveal Byron Bay's predatory 2000s culture
item.description: Rachel Kila has waived her right to anonymity to join dozens of others speaking out about the experiences of young girls in Byron Bay in the early 2000s, where they say it became normalised for older boys and men to groom and have sex with girls then publicly humiliate them.
item.dc:creator: Emily Baker and Hannah Ross

media:group.media:description: Rachel Kila has waived her right to anonymity to speak out against the culture she grew up in.

item.title: Melbourne Fringe board rejects artists' request for 'fair cut'
item.description: An open letter to festival organisers that attracted almost 600 signatures asked them to "give artists a fair cut", with specific requests to change the way fees are calculated.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Alvaro

media:group.media:description: This year's festival is due to run from September 29 to October 18.

item.title: Brazil authorities investigate two possible Ebola cases as outbreak grows
item.description: The true reach of the Ebola outbreak in central Africa could be much wider than the official figures published by central African authorities, the World Health Organization is warning.

media:group.media:description: Health agencies are intensifying efforts to get on top of a growing Ebola outbreak in central Africa.

item.title: Why is Labor spending its political majority on this?
item.description: Why has Labor decided to spend the political capital of a 50-seat majority on a minor change to capital gains tax on businesses?
item.dc:creator: Alan Kohler

media:group.media:description: The loss of housing affordability over the past 25 years has been a double disaster: for those who don't own a house and for many of those who do.

item.title: Quietly published Liberal Party paper raises gender membership concerns
item.description: The Liberal Party has quietly published a paper that floats offering a cut price $10 digital membership to join the party, in an effort to offset its declining numbers.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Karvelas

media:group.media:description: The Liberal Party has quietly published a paper that grapples with the party's gender and age woes.

item.title: Australian housing market stalls in May
item.description: Australia's housing market continues to lose momentum, with national home values flatlining in May as higher interest rates, weak confidence and proposed property tax changes weigh on demand.
item.dc:creator: Lin Lin

media:group.media:description: Home values were flat in May, with the weakness led by Sydney and Melbourne.

item.title: 'Caught lying': Police bashed Brad, then charged him with assaulting them
item.description: After fracturing about a dozen of a man's ribs and puncturing his lung, police officers discussed how to explain what happened to him.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Welch, Amy Greenbank, and Elise Potaka

media:group.media:description: As officers try to get Brad Kellson into a cell, Leading Senior Constable Mark Davis knees him repeatedly.

item.title: Virgin holds $90m in unclaimed COVID flight credits. They expire in a month
item.description: The airline had more than $90 million in unspent travel credits on its books earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Dannielle Maguire

media:group.media:description: Virgin Australia's COVID credits expire at the end of June.

item.title: How Trials turned traumatic childhood into triumphant solo album
item.description: The prolific A.B. Original rapper-producer talks about learning to "marry the wrong feeling with the right music".
item.dc:creator: Al Newstead

media:group.media:description: Multi-award-winning producer, rapper, collaborator and proud Ngarrindjeri man Trials has released his first ever solo project.

item.title: Dozens killed by blast in rebel-held Myanmar village
item.description: Authorities say the blast was likely caused by explosives used for mining that had been stored in a warehouse.

media:group.media:description: The explosion in Kaungtup village flattened homes and has killed dozens of people.

item.title: Inside the new 'ghost proxy' terror group 'we need to take seriously'
item.description: Australia would be the "perfect" target for a fledgling Islamist terror network that has carried out antisemitic attacks in Europe, the UK and Canada, experts have warned.
item.dc:creator: Riley Stuart

media:group.media:description: HAYI has claimed responsibility for an arson attack on these Hatzolah ambulances in London in March.

item.title: Family lawyers say there has been an uptick in 'prenup' interest
item.description: Binding financial agreements take time and money to obtain. Here's how they work.

media:group.media:description: A financial agreement can be made before, during or after a marriage or de facto relationship.

item.title: Quiz: Will you be crowned the history champ this week?
item.description: Who took the first five-wicket haul in one-day cricket? And which state capital was the last to get ABC TV?

media:group.media:description: Miss Australia Jennifer Hawkins (L) reacts after being crowned Miss Universe 2004 by Amelia Vega (R), Miss Universe 2003, in Quito, June 1, 2004. Hawkins, a 20-year old model and dance choreographer, won the title at a dazzling ceremony in Ecuador on Tuesday from a field of 80 beauty queens. Miss USA Shandi Finnessey won second place and Alba Reyes of Puerto Rico, an aspiring doctor, came in third. REUTERS/ GG

item.title: Ian Thorpe's mythic rise from boy to hero had an ironic twist
item.description: Ian Thorpe become a global star as a teenager, attracting both opportunities and scrutiny. Now, he wishes he'd done some things differently.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Purcell

media:group.media:description: In the 200-metre freestyle final at the Athens Games, Ian Thorpe claimed his first Olympic gold for the event.

item.title: Young Australians reject budget as One Nation popularity climbs further
item.description: Labor's federal budget is falling flat with its target demographic as millennials say they would rather vote for One Nation than Labor or the Greens, according to a new Redbridge poll.
item.dc:creator: Sara Tomevska

media:group.media:description: The Redbridge poll found Pauline Hanson was the preferred prime minister among gen X voters.

item.title: Netanyahu orders deeper incursion into Lebanon as Israeli troops seize castle
item.description: Israeli troops have captured a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon, in their deepest incursion into the country in decades.

media:group.media:description: The Israeli capture of the castle and strategic ridge is a major advance against Iran-backed Hezbollah.

item.title: Horses bolt through Rome after police officer sets off fireworks
item.description: Fireworks set off without permission by a police officer during a rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade startled dozens of horses, sending them bolting through the capital and injuring several riders.

media:group.media:description: A horse spooked by a police officer's unauthorised fireworks bolts through Rome.

item.title: Applications to protect Victoria Park rejected ahead of construction of Olympic stadium
item.description: The federal government has rejected two applications for protection of Brisbane's Victoria Park ahead of construction on the Olympic stadium beginning tomorrow, but is still considering another five.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Loram and Claudia Williams

media:group.media:description: Supporters of Victoria Park gathered to oppose the construction of the stadium on Sunday.

item.title: Divers at Laos cave search for two men thought to be trapped further down
item.description: The remaining four men from a group of five were freed from the flooded cave on Saturday evening, in extraordinary and emotional scenes, as search efforts continue for two people thought to still be trapped inside.

media:group.media:description: Rescuers stand under torchlight near the entrance of the cave.

item.title: Why tonight's full moon will be a rare occurrence
item.description: If you look up to the full moon at sunset this evening, you may notice it looks ever so slightly smaller than usual.
item.dc:creator: Dannielle Maguire

media:group.media:description: Tonight's full moon will be a rare occurrence, but won't look all that different.

item.title: West Coast claims fourth win as Essendon's injury nightmare continues
item.description: West Coast makes light work of Essendon at Perth Stadium, as the Bombers lose another promising youngster to a serious injury.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: Harley Reid and West Coast proved far too strong for an undermanned Essendon side that lost another player to injury.

item.title: Australia scraps new Virginia-class submarines in AUKUS shake-up
item.description: The US, UK and Australia have announced they will "streamline" the purchase, with Australia no longer buying new submarines, and acquiring three second-hand ones.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Australia will now buy second-hand Virginia-class submarines.

item.title: Police 'open' to learning from volunteers who found remains in renewed search
item.description: A renewed search by volunteers for missing Tasmanian man Peter Willoughby led to the discovery of remains on Saturday. It's the second time this year a group of volunteers has made a significant find during the search for a missing person.
item.dc:creator: Scout Wallen

media:group.media:description: Volunteer searchers found remains during a renewed search for missing man Peter Willoughby on Saturday.

item.title: Iran's internet revival unearths videos regime 'didn't want world to witness'
item.description: After being cut off from the world for more than 12 weeks, Iranians have come back online. Their initial reactions are joy, shock and confusion.
item.dc:creator: Tavleen Singh

media:group.media:description: Iran’s new-found internet access unleashes anti-regime videos from protests held in January this year
